attack_surface.data_description: Malicious contract artifacts or registry configuration provided to Hyperlane Registry

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: External Github API to Hyperlane Registry

security_gap.gap_description: If malicious contract artifacts or configurations are accepted from a compromised/spoofed Github API and subsequently used, they could execute with unintended privileges within the Hyperlane ecosystem.

actor_risk.description: A compromised or spoofed Github API (Actor 107) could serve malicious artifacts or configurations that, when used by Hyperlane, lead to privilege escalation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Execution of malicious code from untrusted artifacts or use of malicious configurations can lead to full system compromise or unauthorized control.

assumptions: Contract artifacts or registry configurations fetched from the Github API are trusted and used in deployment or operational processes without sufficient independent verification, code review, or sandboxing.

Malicious code embedded in an artifact, or a malicious configuration, can grant attackers elevated privileges or unauthorized capabilities when the artifact is deployed/executed or the configuration is applied.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict validation, integrity checks (e.g., checksums, signature verification), and mandatory code review for all critical artifacts and configurations fetched from external sources like Github API before they are used in deployment or operations. Use sandboxing for execution where feasible.

description: An attacker, by providing malicious contract artifacts (Asset 210) or registry configurations (Asset 208) through a compromised or spoofed Github API feed (Actor 107) via Data Flow 427 to Hyperlane Registry (Component 323), causes these items to be used by Hyperlane, leading to unauthorized privilege escalation within the system.
